title: 1 Chronicles 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Adam, Seth, Enosh,
2 Kenan, Mahalalel, Jared,
3 Enoch, Methuselah, Lamech.
4 The sons of Noah: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
5 The sons of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
6 The sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
7 The sons of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, the Kittites, and the Rodanites.
8 The sons of Ham: Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.
9 The sons of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabta, Raamah, and Sabteca. The sons of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.
10 Cush was the father of Nimrod. Nimrod became a great hero on the earth.
11 Mizraim was the father of the Ludites, the Anamites, the Lehabites, the Naphtuhites,
12 the Pathrusites, the Casluhites (the Philistines came from them), and the Caphtorites.
13 Canaan was the father of Sidon, his oldest son, and of the Hittites,
14 the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
15 the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
16 the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites.
17 The sons of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram. The sons of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Meshech.
18 Arphaxad was the father of Shelah. Shelah was the father of Eber.
19 Eber had two sons. He named one Peleg, because in his time people split the earth. His brother was named Joktan.
20 Joktan was the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
21 Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
22 Obal, Abimael, Sheba,
23 Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were sons of Joktan.
24 So from Shem came Arphaxad, Shelah,
25 Eber, Peleg, Reu,
26 Serug, Nahor, Terah,
27 and Abram (that is, Abraham).
28 The sons of Abraham were Isaac and Ishmael.
29 These are their family lists:
30 Nebaioth was Ishmael's oldest son. Then came Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam, Mishma, Dumah, Massa, Hadad, Tema,
31 Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah. These were the sons of Ishmael.
32 The sons of Keturah, Abraham's second wife: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah. The sons of Jokshan: Sheba and Dedan.
33 The sons of Midian: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All of these were Keturah's sons.
34 Abraham was the father of Isaac. The sons of Isaac: Esau and Israel.
35 The sons of Esau: Eliphaz, Reuel, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
36 The sons of Eliphaz: Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz; and by Timna, Amalek.
37 The sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah.
38 The sons of Seir: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan.
39 The sons of Lotan: Hori and Homam. Timna was Lotan's sister.
40 The sons of Shobal: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam. The sons of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah.
41 The son of Anah: Dishon. The sons of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
42 The sons of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan. The sons of Dishan: Uz and Aran.
43 These are the kings who ruled in the land of Edom before any king ruled over the Israelites: Bela son of Beor. His city was named Dinhabah.
44 When Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah became king after him.
45 When Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites became king after him.
46 When Husham died, Hadad son of Bedad became king after him. He defeated Midian in the country of Moab. His city was named Avith.
47 When Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah became king after him.
48 When Samlah died, Shaul from Rehoboth on the Euphrates became king after him.
49 When Shaul died, Baal-hanan son of Achbor became king after him.
50 When Baal-hanan died, Hadad became king after him. His city was named Pau. His wife's name was Mehetabel daughter of Matred, the daughter of Me-zahab.
51 Then Hadad died. Now the leaders of Edom were Timna, Alvah, Jetheth,
52 Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon,
53 Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar,
54 Magdiel, and Iram. These were the leaders of Edom.

title: 1 Chronicles 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 These were the sons of Israel:
2 Reuben, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, Zebulun, Dan, Joseph, Benjamin, Naphtali, Gad, and Asher.
3 The sons of Judah were Er, Onan, and Shelah. Bath-shua the Canaanite was their mother. Er was Judah's first son. He did evil before the LORD, so the LORD killed him.
4 Tamar was Judah's son's wife. She gave Judah two sons, Perez and Zerah. So Judah had five sons in all.
5 The sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
6 The sons of Zerah were Zimri, Ethan, Heman, Calcol, and Dara — five in all.
7 The son of Carmi was Achar. He brought trouble on Israel by taking things set apart for God.
8 The son of Ethan was Azariah.
9 The sons born to Hezron were Jerahmeel, Ram, and Caleb.
10 Ram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon, a leader of Judah's people.
11 Nahshon was the father of Salmon. Salmon was the father of Boaz.
12 Boaz was the father of Obed. Obed was the father of Jesse.
13 Jesse was the father of Eliab, his oldest son. Abinadab was the second son. Shimea was the third.
14 Nethanel was the fourth son. Raddai was the fifth.
15 Ozem was the sixth son. David was the seventh.
16 Their sisters were Zeruiah and Abigail. The three sons of Zeruiah were Abishai, Joab, and Asahel.
17 Abigail was the mother of Amasa. Amasa's father was Jether the Ishmaelite.
18 Caleb son of Hezron had children by his wife Azubah and by Jerioth. The sons of Azubah were Jesher, Shobab, and Ardon.
19 When Azubah died, Caleb married Ephrath. She gave him a son, Hur.
20 Hur was the father of Uri. Uri was the father of Bezalel.
21 Later, Hezron married the daughter of Machir, the father of Gilead. He was sixty years old then. She gave him a son, Segub.
22 Segub was the father of Jair. Jair had twenty-three cities in the land of Gilead.
23 But Geshur and Aram captured Havvoth-jair. They also took Kenath and its sixty nearby villages. All these were descendants of Machir, the father of Gilead.
24 After Hezron died in Caleb-ephrathah, his wife Abijah gave him a son, Ashhur. Ashhur was the father of Tekoa.
25 The sons of Jerahmeel, the oldest son of Hezron, were these:
26 Ram his oldest son, then Bunah, Oren, Ozem, and Ahijah. Jerahmeel had another wife named Atarah. She was the mother of Onam.
27 The sons of Ram, the oldest son of Jerahmeel, were Maaz, Jamin, and Eker.
28 The sons of Onam were Shammai and Jada. The sons of Shammai were these:
29 Nadab and Abishur. Abishur's wife was named Abihail. She gave him Ahban and Molid.
30 The sons of Nadab were Seled and Appaim. Seled died without children.
31 The son of Appaim was Ishi. The son of Ishi was Sheshan. The son of Sheshan was Ahlai.
32 The sons of Jada, the brother of Shammai, were Jether and Jonathan. Jether died without children.
33 The sons of Jonathan were Peleth and Zaza. These were the descendants of Jerahmeel.
34 Sheshan had no sons, only daughters. But he had an Egyptian servant named Jarha.
35 Sheshan gave his daughter in marriage to his servant Jarha. She gave Jarha a son, Attai.
36 Attai was the father of Nathan. Nathan was the father of Zabad.
37 Zabad was the father of Ephlal. Ephlal was the father of Obed.
38 Obed was the father of Jehu. Jehu was the father of Azariah.
39 Azariah was the father of Helez. Helez was the father of Elasah.
40 Elasah was the father of Sismai. Sismai was the father of Shallum.
41 Shallum was the father of Jekamiah. Jekamiah was the father of Elishama.
42 The sons of Caleb, the brother of Jerahmeel, were these. Mesha was his oldest son and the father of Ziph. Mareshah was his second son and the father of Hebron.
43 The sons of Hebron were these:
44 Korah, Tappuah, Rekem, and Shema. Shema was the father of Raham. Raham was the father of Jorkeam. Rekem was the father of Shammai.
45 The son of Shammai was Maon. Maon was the father of Beth-zur.
46 Caleb's second wife Ephah was the mother of Haran, Moza, and Gazez. Haran was the father of Gazez.
47 The sons of Jahdai were Regem, Jotham, Geshan, Pelet, Ephah, and Shaaph.
48 Caleb's second wife Maacah was the mother of Sheber and Tirhanah.
49 She was also the mother of Shaaph, the father of Madmannah. She was the mother of Sheva, the father of Machbenah and Gibea. Caleb's daughter was Acsah.
50 These were the descendants of Caleb. The sons of Hur, the oldest son of Ephrathah, were these:
51 Shobal the father of Kiriath-jearim, Salma the father of Bethlehem, and Hareph the father of Beth-gader.
52 These were the descendants of Shobal the father of Kiriath-jearim:
53 Haroeh, half the Manahathites, and the family groups of Kiriath-jearim. They were the Ithrites, Puthites, Shumathites, and Mishraites. From them came the Zorathites and Eshtaolites.
54 The descendants of Salma were these:

55 Bethlehem, the Netophathites, Atroth-beth-joab, half the Manahathites, and the Zorites. They also included the scribe family groups who lived at Jabez — the Tirathites, Shimeathites, and Sucathites. These are the Kenites who came from Hammath, the father of the house of Rechab.

title: 1 Chronicles 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 These were the sons of David, born to him in Hebron: The first son was Amnon. His mother was Ahinoam from Jezreel. The second son was Daniel. His mother was Abigail from Carmel.
2 The third son was Absalom. His mother was Maacah, daughter of King Talmai of Geshur. The fourth son was Adonijah. His mother was Haggith.
3 The fifth son was Shephatiah. His mother was Abital. The sixth son was Ithream. His mother was David's wife Eglah.
4 These six sons were born to David in Hebron. There he ruled as king for seven years and six months. Then David ruled in Jerusalem for thirty-three years.
5 These sons were born to him in Jerusalem: Shimea, Shobab, Nathan, and Solomon. Their mother was Bathsheba, daughter of Ammiel.
6 David's other sons were Ibhar, Elishua, Eliphelet,
7 Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
8 Elishama, Eliada, and Eliphelet — nine sons in all.
9 These were all the sons of David. He also had sons by his second wives. Tamar was their sister.
10 Solomon's son was Rehoboam.
11 Abijah was his son. Asa was his son. Jehoshaphat was his son. Joram was his son. Ahaziah was his son. Joash was his son.
12 Amaziah was his son. Azariah was his son. Jotham was his son.
13 Ahaz was his son. Hezekiah was his son. Manasseh was his son.
14 Amon was his son. Josiah was his son.
15 The sons of Josiah were these: Johanan was the first son. Jehoiakim was the second. Zedekiah was the third. Shallum was the fourth.
16 The kings after Jehoiakim were these: Jeconiah his son, and Zedekiah.
17 The sons of Jeconiah the prisoner were these:
18 Shealtiel his son, Malchiram, Pedaiah, Shenazzar, Jekamiah, Hoshama, and Nedabiah.
19 The sons of Pedaiah were Zerubbabel and Shimei. The children of Zerubbabel were these:
20 Meshullam and Hananiah, and their sister Shelomith. There were five more sons: Hashubah, Ohel, Berechiah, Hasadiah, and Jushab-hesed.
21 The sons of Hananiah were Pelatiah and Jeshaiah. He also had the sons of Rephaiah, of Arnan, of Obadiah, and of Shecaniah.
22 The six sons of Shecaniah were these: Shemaiah and his sons Hattush, Igal, Bariah, Neariah, and Shaphat.
23 The sons of Neariah were Elioenai, Hizkiah, and Azrikam — three in all.
24 The sons of Elioenai were Hodaviah, Eliashib, Pelaiah, Akkub, Johanan, Delaiah, and Anani — seven in all.

title: 1 Chronicles 4
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 The descendants of Judah were Perez, Hezron, Carmi, Hur, and Shobal.
2 Reaiah son of Shobal was the father of Jahath. Jahath was the father of Ahumai and Lahad. These were the family groups of the Zorathites.
3 These were the sons of Etam: Jezreel, Ishma, and Idbash. Their sister was named Hazzelelponi.
4 Penuel was the father of Gedor. Ezer was the father of Hushah. These were the descendants of Hur, the oldest son of Ephrathah and the father of Bethlehem.
5 Ashhur the father of Tekoa had two wives, Helah and Naarah.
6 Naarah gave him Ahuzzam, Hepher, Temeni, and Haahashtari. These were the descendants of Naarah.
7 The sons of Helah were Zereth, Zohar, Ethnan,
8 and Koz. Koz was the father of Anub and Zobebah, and of the family groups of Aharhel son of Harum.
9 Now Jabez was more respected than his brothers. His mother had named him Jabez. She said, "I had much pain when he was born."

10 Jabez prayed to the God of Israel. He said, "Oh, that you would bless me and enlarge my territory! Let your hand be with me, and keep me from harm so that I will be free from pain." And God gave him what he asked for.
11 Chelub the brother of Shuhah was the father of Mehir. Mehir was the father of Eshton.
12 Eshton was the father of Beth-rapha, Paseah, and Tehinnah the father of Ir-nahash. These were the men of Recah.
13 The sons of Kenaz were Othniel and Seraiah. The sons of Othniel were Hathath and Meonothai.
14 Meonothai was the father of Ophrah. Seraiah was the father of Joab, the father of those living in Ge-harashim. The place got this name because its people were skilled workers.
15 The sons of Caleb son of Jephunneh were Iru, Elah, and Naam. The son of Elah was Kenaz.
16 The sons of Jehallelel were Ziph, Ziphah, Tiria, and Asarel.
17 The sons of Ezrah were Jether, Mered, Epher, and Jalon.

18 Mered's wife Bithiah gave birth to Miriam, Shammai, and Ishbah the father of Eshtemoa. These were the children of Pharaoh's daughter Bithiah. Mered also took a Judean wife. She gave birth to Jered the father of Gedor, Heber the father of Soco, and Jekuthiel the father of Zanoah.
19 The sons of Hodiah's wife, the sister of Naham, were the fathers of Keilah the Garmite and of Eshtemoa the Maacathite.
20 The sons of Shimon were Amnon, Rinnah, Ben-hanan, and Tilon. The descendants of Ishi were Zoheth and Ben-zoheth.
21 The sons of Shelah son of Judah were these:

22 Er the father of Lecah, Laadah the father of Mareshah and the family groups of the linen workers at Beth-ashbea, Jokim, the men of Cozeba, and Joash and Saraph, who ruled in Moab and Jashubi-lehem. (These names come from old records.)
23 These were the potters who lived at Netaim and Gederah. They lived there and worked for the king.
24 The descendants of Simeon were Nemuel, Jamin, Jarib, Zerah, and Shaul.
25 The sons of Shaul were Shallum, Mibsam, and Mishma.
26 The sons of Mishma were Hammuel, Zaccur, and Shimei.
27 Shimei had sixteen sons and six daughters. But his brothers did not have many children. So their whole family group did not grow as large as the sons of Judah.
28 They lived in Beersheba, Moladah, Hazar-shual,
29 Bilhah, Ezem, Tolad,
30 Bethuel, Hormah, Ziklag,
31 Beth-marcaboth, Hazar-susim, Beth-biri, and Shaaraim. These were their cities until the time when David was king.
32 Their villages were Etam, Ain, Rimmon, Tochen, and Ashan — five towns —
33 and all the small villages around them as far as Baal. These were their homes, and they kept a family record:
34 Meshobab, Jamlech, Joshah son of Amaziah,
35 Joel, Jehu son of Joshibiah (son of Seraiah, son of Asiel),
36 Elioenai, Jaakobah, Jeshohaiah, Asaiah, Adiel, Jesimiel, Benaiah,
37 and Ziza son of Shiphi (son of Allon, son of Jedaiah, son of Shimri, son of Shemaiah).
38 These men listed by name were the leaders of their family groups. Their families grew very large.
39 They went to the entrance of Gedor, to the east side of the valley. They were looking for grass for their flocks.
40 There they found rich, good grass. The land was wide and open, peaceful, and quiet. Some Hamites had lived there before.

41 These men listed by name came in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah. They attacked the Hamites and Meunites who lived there. They wiped them out completely, and it is so even now. Then they settled in their place, because there was grass for their flocks.
42 Five hundred of these Simeonites went to Mount Seir. Their leaders were Pelatiah, Neariah, Rephaiah, and Uzziel, the sons of Ishi.
43 They struck down the few Amalekites who were left and had escaped. They have lived there even to this day.

title: 1 Chronicles 5
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 These were the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel. He was the first son. But his birthright went to the sons of Joseph son of Israel. Reuben had shamed his father's bed. So he is not listed as the firstborn.
2 Judah grew stronger than his brothers, and a ruler came from him. But the birthright belonged to Joseph.
3 The sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
4 The family of Joel:
5 Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son, Micah his son, Reaiah his son, Baal his son,
6 and Beerah his son. Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria took Beerah away as a prisoner.
7 Beerah was a leader of the Reubenites. His family groups are written in their family record:
8 Jeiel the chief, Zechariah, and Bela son of Azaz, the son of Shema, the son of Joel. They lived in Aroer and as far as Nebo and Baal-meon.
9 They also lived in the east as far as the edge of the desert. The desert reaches the Euphrates River. Their farm animals had grown in number in the land of Gilead.
10 In the days of Saul they fought against the Hagrites. They defeated them. Then they took the homes of the Hagrites east of Gilead.
11 The family of Gad lived next to the Reubenites in the land of Bashan, as far as Salecah:
12 Joel was the chief, Shapham the second, then Jaanai and Shaphat. They lived in Bashan.
13 Their family members were Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jacan, Zia, and Eber — seven in all.
14 These were the sons of Abihail son of Huri, the son of Jaroah, the son of Gilead, the son of Michael, the son of Jeshishai, the son of Jahdo, the son of Buz.
15 Ahi son of Abdiel, the son of Guni, was the head of their family.
16 They lived in Gilead, in Bashan and its towns, and in all the grasslands of Sharon.
17 All of them were listed in the family records. This was when Jotham was king of Judah and Jeroboam was king of Israel.
18 The Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh had 44,760 warriors. They were brave men. They carried the shield and sword. They shot the bow. They were ready for battle.
19 They fought against the Hagrites, and against Jetur, Naphish, and Nodab.
20 They cried out to God in battle. God helped them against their enemies. The Hagrites and all their friends were given into their hands. They trusted God, and He answered their prayers.
21 They took the farm animals of the Hagrites — 50,000 camels, 250,000 sheep, and 2,000 donkeys. They also took 100,000 prisoners.
22 Many others died, because the battle belonged to God. They lived in the land until the exile.
23 The people of the half-tribe of Manasseh were many. They lived in the land from Bashan to Baal-hermon (that is, Senir, also called Mount Hermon).
24 These were the heads of their families: Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel. They were strong and brave men, famous men, and heads of their families.
25 But they were not faithful to the God of their fathers. They worshiped the false gods of the peoples of the land. God had destroyed those peoples before them.

26 So the God of Israel moved the heart of Pul king of Assyria (that is, Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria). He took the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh into exile. He brought them to Halah, Habor, Hara, and the river of Gozan. They are still there until today.

title: 1 Chronicles 6
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 The sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
2 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
3 The children of Amram: Aaron, Moses, and Miriam. The sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
4 Eleazar was the father of Phinehas. Phinehas was the father of Abishua.
5 Abishua was the father of Bukki. Bukki was the father of Uzzi.
6 Uzzi was the father of Zerahiah. Zerahiah was the father of Meraioth.
7 Meraioth was the father of Amariah. Amariah was the father of Ahitub.
8 Ahitub was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Ahimaaz.
9 Ahimaaz was the father of Azariah. Azariah was the father of Johanan.
10 Johanan was the father of Azariah. Azariah served as priest in the temple that Solomon built in Jerusalem.
11 Azariah was the father of Amariah. Amariah was the father of Ahitub.
12 Ahitub was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Shallum.
13 Shallum was the father of Hilkiah. Hilkiah was the father of Azariah.
14 Azariah was the father of Seraiah. Seraiah was the father of Jehozadak.
15 Jehozadak was taken away as a prisoner. The LORD sent Judah and Jerusalem into exile by Nebuchadnezzar.
16 The sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
17 These are the names of the sons of Gershom: Libni and Shimei.
18 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel.
19 The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are the clans of the Levites by their fathers:
20 Of Gershom:
21 Libni his son, Jahath his son, Zimmah his son, Joah his son, Iddo his son, Zerah his son, and Jeatherai his son.
22 The descendants of Kohath:
23 Amminadab his son, Korah his son, Assir his son, Elkanah his son, Ebiasaph his son, Assir his son,
24 Tahath his son, Uriel his son, Uzziah his son, and Shaul his son.
25 The descendants of Elkanah:
26 Amasai, Ahimoth, Elkanah his son, Zophai his son, Nahath his son,
27 Eliab his son, Jeroham his son, and Elkanah his son.
28 The sons of Samuel: Joel his firstborn and Abijah his second son.
29 The descendants of Merari:
30 Mahli, Libni his son, Shimei his son, Uzzah his son, Shimea his son, Haggiah his son, and Asaiah his son.
31 These are the men David put in charge of the music in the house of the LORD after the ark rested there.
32 They served with song before the tabernacle, the Tent of Meeting. They did this until Solomon built the house of the LORD in Jerusalem. They did their work by the rules given to them.
33 These are the men who served, with their sons. From the Kohathites:
34 Heman the singer, the son of Joel, the son of Samuel, the son of Elkanah, the son of Jeroham, the son of Eliel, the son of Toah,
35 the son of Zuph, the son of Elkanah, the son of Mahath, the son of Amasai,
36 the son of Elkanah, the son of Joel, the son of Azariah, the son of Zephaniah,
37 the son of Tahath, the son of Assir, the son of Ebiasaph, the son of Korah,
38 the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, the son of Israel.
39 Heman's relative was Asaph, who served at his right side:
40 Asaph the son of Berechiah, the son of Shimea, the son of Michael, the son of Baaseiah, the son of Malchijah,
41 the son of Ethni, the son of Zerah, the son of Adaiah,
42 the son of Ethan, the son of Zimmah, the son of Shimei,
43 the son of Jahath, the son of Gershom, the son of Levi.
44 On the left side were their relatives, the sons of Merari:
45 Ethan the son of Kishi, the son of Abdi, the son of Malluch, the son of Hashabiah, the son of Amaziah, the son of Hilkiah,
46 the son of Amzi, the son of Bani, the son of Shemer,
47 the son of Mahli, the son of Mushi, the son of Merari, the son of Levi.
48 Their fellow Levites were given every kind of service for the tabernacle, the house of God.

49 But Aaron and his sons did all the work of the Most Holy Place. They gave the offerings on the altar of burnt offering and on the altar of incense. They did this to make atonement for Israel. They followed all that Moses the servant of God had commanded.
50 These were the descendants of Aaron:
51 Eleazar his son, Phinehas his son, Abishua his son, Bukki his son, Uzzi his son, Zerahiah his son,
52 Meraioth his son, Amariah his son, Ahitub his son,
53 Zadok his son, and Ahimaaz his son.
54 Now these were the lands given to the descendants of Aaron from the Kohathite clan. They were given homes because the first lot fell to them:
55 They were given Hebron in the land of Judah and the pasturelands around it.
56 But the fields and villages around the city were given to Caleb son of Jephunneh.
57 So the descendants of Aaron were given Hebron (a city of refuge), Libnah, Jattir, Eshtemoa,
58 Hilen, Debir,
59 Ashan, Juttah, and Beth-shemesh, with their pasturelands.
60 And from the tribe of Benjamin they were given Gibeon, Geba, Alemeth, and Anathoth, with their pasturelands. They had thirteen cities in all among their families.
61 The rest of the Kohathites were given ten cities by lot from the half-tribe of Manasseh.
62 The Gershomites, by their clans, were given thirteen cities by lot. These came from the tribes of Issachar, Asher, Naphtali, and Manasseh in Bashan.
63 The Merarites, by their families, were given twelve cities by lot. These came from the tribes of Reuben, Gad, and Zebulun.
64 So the Israelites gave the Levites these cities and their pasturelands.
65 They gave by lot the cities named above from the tribes of Judah, Simeon, and Benjamin.
66 And some of the clans of the Kohathites were given cities from the tribe of Ephraim:
67 They were given Shechem (a city of refuge) with its pasturelands in the hill country of Ephraim, and Gezer,
68 Jokmeam, Beth-horon,
69 Aijalon, and Gath-rimmon, with their pasturelands.
70 And from the half-tribe of Manasseh the remaining clans of the Kohathites were given Aner and Bileam, with their pasturelands.
71 The Gershomites received the following: From the clan of the half-tribe of Manasseh they were given Golan in Bashan and also Ashtaroth, with their pasturelands.
72 From the tribe of Issachar they were given Kedesh, Daberath,
73 Ramoth, and Anem, with their pasturelands.
74 From the tribe of Asher they were given Mashal, Abdon,
75 Hukok, and Rehob, with their pasturelands.
76 And from the tribe of Naphtali they were given Kedesh in Galilee, Hammon, and Kiriathaim, with their pasturelands.
77 The Merarites (the rest of the Levites) received the following: From the tribe of Zebulun they were given Rimmono and Tabor, with their pasturelands.
78 From the tribe of Reuben east of the Jordan, across from Jericho, they were given Bezer in the wilderness, Jahzah,
79 Kedemoth, and Mephaath, with their pasturelands.
80 And from the tribe of Gad they were given Ramoth in Gilead, Mahanaim,
81 Heshbon, and Jazer, with their pasturelands.

title: 1 Chronicles 7
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 The sons of Issachar were Tola, Puah, Jashub, and Shimron — four in all.

2 The sons of Tola were Uzzi, Rephaiah, Jeriel, Jahmai, Ibsam, and Shemuel. They were the leaders of their families. In the days of David, the family records listed 22,600 brave fighters from Tola's line.
3 The son of Uzzi was Izrahiah. The sons of Izrahiah were:
4 Michael, Obadiah, Joel, and Isshiah. All five were chiefs. Their records showed 36,000 troops ready for war, because they had many wives and children.
5 All the families of Issachar had 87,000 brave fighters in total. The family records listed them all.
6 The three sons of Benjamin were Bela, Becher, and Jediael.
7 The sons of Bela were Ezbon, Uzzi, Uzziel, Jerimoth, and Iri — five family leaders in all. The records listed 22,034 brave fighters from them.
8 The sons of Becher were:
9 Zemirah, Joash, Eliezer, Elioenai, Omri, Jeremoth, Abijah, Anathoth, and Alemeth. All these were sons of Becher. The records listed 20,200 brave family leaders.
10 The son of Jediael was Bilhan. The sons of Bilhan were:
11 Jeush, Benjamin, Ehud, Chenaanah, Zethan, Tarshish, and Ahishahar. All these sons of Jediael were family leaders and brave fighters. They had 17,200 men ready for war.
12 The Shuppites and Huppites came from Ir. The Hushites came from Aher.
13 The sons of Naphtali were Jahziel, Guni, Jezer, and Shallum. They came from the line of Bilhah.
14 The descendants of Manasseh came through his Aramean second wife. They were Asriel and Machir, the father of Gilead.
15 Machir took a wife from the Huppites and Shuppites. His sister was named Maacah. Another descendant was Zelophehad, who had only daughters.
16 Machir's wife Maacah gave birth to a son. She named him Peresh. His brother was Sheresh, and his sons were Ulam and Rekem.
17 The son of Ulam was Bedan. These were the sons of Gilead son of Machir, son of Manasseh.
18 His sister Hammolecheth gave birth to Ishhod, Abiezer, and Mahlah.
19 The sons of Shemida were Ahian, Shechem, Likhi, and Aniam.
20 The descendants of Ephraim were:

21 Shuthelah, his son Bered, his son Tahath, his son Eleadah, his son Tahath, his son Zabad, and his son Shuthelah. The natives of Gath killed Ezer and Elead, because they went down to steal their farm animals.
22 Their father Ephraim mourned for many days. His relatives came to comfort him.
23 Then he slept with his wife again. She became pregnant and gave birth to a son. He named the boy Beriah, because trouble had hit his family.
24 His daughter was Sheerah. She built Lower and Upper Beth-horon, and also Uzzen-sheerah.
25 His other sons in the line were Rephah, his son Resheph, his son Telah, his son Tahan,
26 his son Ladan, his son Ammihud, his son Elishama,
27 his son Nun, and his son Joshua.
28 Their land and towns included Bethel and its villages, Naaran to the east, Gezer and its villages to the west, and Shechem and its villages out to Ayyah.
29 Along the borders of Manasseh were Beth-shean, Taanach, Megiddo, and Dor, with their villages. The descendants of Joseph son of Israel lived in these towns.
30 The children of Asher were Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, and Beriah, and their sister Serah.
31 The sons of Beriah were Heber and Malchiel. Malchiel was the father of Birzaith.
32 Heber was the father of Japhlet, Shomer, and Hotham, and of their sister Shua.
33 The sons of Japhlet were Pasach, Bimhal, and Ashvath. These were Japhlet's sons.
34 The sons of Shemer were Ahi, Rohgah, Hubbah, and Aram.
35 The sons of his brother Helem were Zophah, Imna, Shelesh, and Amal.
36 The sons of Zophah were:
37 Suah, Harnepher, Shual, Beri, Imrah, Bezer, Hod, Shamma, Shilshah, Ithran, and Beera.
38 The sons of Jether were Jephunneh, Pispa, and Ara.
39 The sons of Ulla were Arah, Hanniel, and Rizia.
40 All these were the descendants of Asher. They were family leaders, strong and brave fighters, and top leaders. The family records listed 26,000 men ready for war.

title: 1 Chronicles 8
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Benjamin was the father of Bela, his firstborn son. Ashbel was the second son. Aharah was the third.
2 Nohah was the fourth, and Rapha was the fifth.
3 The sons of Bela were these:
4 Addar, Gera, Abihud, Abishua, Naaman, Ahoah,
5 Gera, Shephuphan, and Huram.
6 These were the descendants of Ehud. They were family leaders in Geba. Later they were forced to leave their home and go to Manahath:
7 Naaman, Ahijah, and Gera. Gera made them leave. He was the father of Uzza and Ahihud.
8 Shaharaim had sons in the land of Moab. First he sent his wives Hushim and Baara away.
9 His wife Hodesh gave him these sons:
10 Jobab, Zibia, Mesha, Malcam, Jeuz, Sachia, and Mirmah. These sons were family leaders.
11 He also had sons by Hushim: Abitub and Elpaal.
12 The sons of Elpaal were these:
13 Eber, Misham, and Shemed, who built Ono and Lod and their villages. Beriah and Shema were also his sons. They were family leaders of the people of Aijalon. They forced out the people of Gath.
14 Ahio, Shashak, Jeremoth,
15 Zebadiah, Arad, Eder,
16 Michael, Ishpah, and Joha were the sons of Beriah.
17 Zebadiah, Meshullam, Hizki, Heber,
18 Ishmerai, Izliah, and Jobab were the sons of Elpaal.
19 Jakim, Zichri, Zabdi,
20 Elienai, Zillethai, Eliel,
21 Adaiah, Beraiah, and Shimrath were the sons of Shimei.
22 Ishpan, Eber, Eliel,
23 Abdon, Zichri, Hanan,
24 Hananiah, Elam, Anthothijah,
25 Iphdeiah, and Penuel were the sons of Shashak.
26 Shamsherai, Shehariah, Athaliah,
27 Jaareshiah, Elijah, and Zichri were the sons of Jeroham.
28 All these men were family leaders. Their family records list them as chiefs. They lived in Jerusalem.
29 Jeiel was the father of Gibeon. He lived in Gibeon. His wife's name was Maacah.
30 Abdon was his firstborn son. Then came Zur, Kish, Baal, Nadab,
31 Gedor, Ahio, Zecher,
32 and Mikloth, who was the father of Shimeah. They also lived near their relatives in Jerusalem.
33 Ner was the father of Kish. Kish was the father of Saul. Saul was the father of Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal.
34 The son of Jonathan was Merib-baal. Merib-baal was the father of Micah.
35 The sons of Micah were Pithon, Melech, Tarea, and Ahaz.
36 Ahaz was the father of Jehoaddah. Jehoaddah was the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri. Zimri was the father of Moza.
37 Moza was the father of Binea. Raphah was his son. Eleasah was his son. Azel was his son.
38 Azel had six sons. Their names were Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. All these were the sons of Azel.
39 The sons of his brother Eshek were these:

40 Ulam was his firstborn son. Jeush was the second. Eliphelet was the third. The sons of Ulam were strong and brave fighters. They were men who shot bows. They had many sons and grandsons — 150 in all. All these were the descendants of Benjamin.

title: 1 Chronicles 9
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 All Israel was written down in the family lists. These lists were in the Book of the Kings of Israel. But Judah was taken away to Babylon because they turned away from God.
2 The first people to move back to their own land were Israelites, priests, Levites, and temple workers.
3 Some of these were from Judah, Benjamin, Ephraim, and Manasseh. They lived in Jerusalem:
4 Uthai son of Ammihud, the son of Omri, the son of Imri, the son of Bani. He was from the family of Perez son of Judah.
5 From the Shilonites: Asaiah the oldest son, and his sons.
6 From the Zerahites: Jeuel, and 690 of his family members.
7 From the Benjamites: Sallu son of Meshullam, the son of Hodaviah, the son of Hassenuah;
8 Ibneiah son of Jeroham; Elah son of Uzzi, the son of Michri; Meshullam son of Shephatiah, the son of Reuel, the son of Ibnijah;
9 and 956 of their family members in their family list. All these men were family leaders.
10 From the priests: Jedaiah, Jehoiarib, and Jachin;
11 Azariah son of Hilkiah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Zadok, the son of Meraioth, the son of Ahitub. He was the chief leader of God's temple.
12 Adaiah son of Jeroham, the son of Pashhur, the son of Malchijah; Maasai son of Adiel, the son of Jahzerah, the son of Meshullam, the son of Meshillemith, the son of Immer;
13 and 1,760 of their family members. They were family leaders and strong men who served in God's temple.
14 From the Levites: Shemaiah son of Hasshub, the son of Azrikam, the son of Hashabiah. He was from the family of Merari.
15 Bakbakkar, Heresh, Galal, and Mattaniah son of Mica, the son of Zichri, the son of Asaph;
16 Obadiah son of Shemaiah, the son of Galal, the son of Jeduthun; and Berechiah son of Asa, the son of Elkanah. Berechiah lived in the villages of the Netophathites.
17 These were the gatekeepers: Shallum, Akkub, Talmon, Ahiman, and their family members.
18 Shallum was their chief. Before, he stood on duty at the King's Gate on the east side. These were the gatekeepers from the camp of the Levites.

19 Shallum son of Kore, the son of Ebiasaph, the son of Korah, and his family from the Korahites had a duty. They guarded the doors of the Tent. Their fathers had also guarded the entrance to the house of the LORD.
20 Long ago, Phinehas son of Eleazar led the gatekeepers. And the LORD was with him.
21 Zechariah son of Meshelemiah was the gatekeeper at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
22 In total, 212 men were chosen as gatekeepers at the doors. They were written down in family lists in their villages. David and Samuel the prophet gave them these trusted jobs.
23 So they and their children guarded the gates of the house of the LORD — the house called the Tent.
24 The gatekeepers stood on duty on four sides: east, west, north, and south.
25 Their family members came from their villages at set times. They served with them for seven days.
26 But the four chief gatekeepers were Levites. They watched over the rooms and treasure rooms of God's temple.
27 They stayed near the temple at night. They guarded it and opened it every morning.
28 Some of them watched over the items used in worship. They counted them when they were brought in or taken out.
29 Others watched over the furniture and other items of the holy place. They also watched over the good flour, wine, oil, frankincense, and spices.
30 Some sons of the priests mixed the spices.
31 A Levite named Mattithiah was the oldest son of Shallum the Korahite. He had the job of baking the bread.
32 Some of their Kohathite brothers prepared the rows of the special bread every Sabbath.
33 The musicians were Levite family leaders. They stayed in the temple rooms. They were free from other work because they served day and night.
34 All these were Levite family leaders, chiefs in their family lists. They lived in Jerusalem.
35 Jeiel the father of Gibeon lived in Gibeon. His wife's name was Maacah.
36 Abdon was his oldest son, then Zur, Kish, Baal, Ner, Nadab,
37 Gedor, Ahio, Zechariah, and Mikloth.
38 Mikloth was the father of Shimeam. They also lived near their family members in Jerusalem.
39 Ner was the father of Kish. Kish was the father of Saul. Saul was the father of Jonathan, Malchishua, Abinadab, and Esh-baal.
40 The son of Jonathan: Merib-baal, who was the father of Micah.
41 The sons of Micah: Pithon, Melech, Tahrea, and Ahaz.
42 Ahaz was the father of Jarah. Jarah was the father of Alemeth, Azmaveth, and Zimri. Zimri was the father of Moza.
43 Moza was the father of Binea. Rephaiah was his son. Elasah was his son. Azel was his son.
44 Azel had six sons. Their names were: Azrikam, Bocheru, Ishmael, Sheariah, Obadiah, and Hanan. These were the sons of Azel.

title: 1 Chronicles 10
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 The Philistines fought against Israel. The men of Israel ran away from them. Many were killed on Mount Gilboa.
2 The Philistines chased Saul and his sons closely. They killed Saul’s sons Jonathan, Abinadab, and Malchishua.
3 The fight became fierce against Saul. The bowmen caught up with him and hurt him.

4 Then Saul said to his armor-bearer, “Take your sword and push it through me. If you don’t, these pagan men will come and hurt me badly!” But his armor-bearer was very afraid and would not do it. So Saul took his own sword and fell on it.
5 When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead, he also fell on his own sword and died.
6 So Saul died with his three sons. All his house died with him.

7 All the Israelites in the valley saw that the army had run away. They also saw that Saul and his sons had died. So they left their cities and ran away. Then the Philistines came and took their cities.
8 The next day, the Philistines came to take things from the dead. They found Saul and his sons fallen on Mount Gilboa.
9 They took Saul’s clothes and armor. They cut off his head. Then they sent messengers all through the land of the Philistines. They told the news in the temple of their idols and among their people.
10 They put his armor in the temple of their gods. They hung his head in the temple of Dagon.
11 The people of Jabesh-gilead heard everything the Philistines had done to Saul.
12 All their brave men went out. They brought back the bodies of Saul and his sons to Jabesh. They buried their bones under the oak tree in Jabesh. Then they did not eat for seven days.
13 So Saul died because he was not faithful to the LORD. He did not keep the word of the LORD. He even asked a spirit-talker for help,
14 and he did not ask the LORD. So the LORD put him to death. He gave the kingdom to David son of Jesse.

title: 1 Chronicles 11
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then all Israel came together to David at Hebron. They said, "Look, we are your own family.

2 Even before now, when Saul was king, you led Israel out to war and brought them home. And the LORD your God said to you, 'You will care for My people Israel like a shepherd, and you will rule over them.'"

3 So all the elders of Israel came to the king at Hebron. There David made a covenant (promise) with them before the LORD. They poured oil on him to make him king over Israel, just as the LORD had said through Samuel.
4 Then David and all Israel marched to Jerusalem (also called Jebus). The Jebusites lived in that land.
5 The people of Jebus said to David, "You will never get in here." But David captured the strong fort of Zion, which is now called the City of David.
6 David had said, "The first man to strike down a Jebusite will be the chief commander." Joab son of Zeruiah went up first, so he became the chief.
7 So David lived in the fort, and that is why they called it the City of David.
8 He built up the city all around it, from the lower walls to the outer wall. Joab fixed the rest of the city.
9 And David became greater and greater, because the LORD of Hosts was with him.
10 These were the chiefs of David's strong fighters. They, with all Israel, helped make his kingdom strong, just as the LORD had said about Israel.
11 This is the list of David's strong fighters: Jashobeam son of Hachmoni was chief of the officers. He fought with his spear against three hundred men and killed them all at one time.
12 Next in rank was Eleazar son of Dodo the Ahohite. He was one of the three strong fighters.
13 He was with David at Pas-dammim when the Philistines came together for battle. There was a field full of barley, and the soldiers ran away from the Philistines.
14 But Eleazar and David stood firm in the middle of the field and kept it safe. They struck down the Philistines, and the LORD won a great victory.
15 Three of the thirty chiefs went down to David, to the rock at the cave of Adullam. A group of Philistines was camped in the Valley of Rephaim.
16 At that time David was in the strong fort, and the Philistine camp was at Bethlehem.
17 David wanted some water very much. He said, "Oh, if only someone would bring me a drink from the well near the gate of Bethlehem!"
18 So the Three broke through the Philistine camp. They drew water from the well at the gate of Bethlehem and brought it to David. But he would not drink it. Instead, he poured it out to the LORD.

19 He said, "My God, I must never do this! How can I drink the blood of these men who risked their lives?" Because they brought it by risking their lives, David would not drink it. These were the brave deeds of the three strong fighters.
20 Abishai, the brother of Joab, was chief of the Three. He lifted his spear against three hundred men and killed them, and he won a great name with the Three.
21 He was given twice as much honor as the rest of the Three, and he became their commander. But he was not counted among the Three.
22 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was a brave man from Kabzeel, and he did many brave deeds. He struck down two top fighters of Moab. On a snowy day he also went down into a pit and killed a lion.

23 He killed an Egyptian too, a huge man five cubits tall. The Egyptian had a spear like a weaver's beam in his hand. But Benaiah went at him with only a club. He grabbed the spear from the man's hand and killed him with his own spear.
24 These were the brave deeds of Benaiah son of Jehoiada, who won a name with the three strong fighters.
25 He was given more honor than the Thirty, but he did not become one of the Three. David put him in charge of his guard.
26 These were the strong fighters: Asahel the brother of Joab, Elhanan son of Dodo of Bethlehem,
27 Shammoth the Harorite, Helez the Pelonite,
28 Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite, Abiezer the Anathothite,
29 Sibbecai the Hushathite, Ilai the Ahohite,
30 Maharai the Netophathite, Heled son of Baanah the Netophathite,
31 Ithai son of Ribai from Gibeah of the Benjamites, Benaiah the Pirathonite,
32 Hurai from the brooks of Gaash, Abiel the Arbathite,
33 Azmaveth the Baharumite, Eliahba the Shaalbonite,
34 the sons of Hashem the Gizonite, Jonathan son of Shagee the Hararite,
35 Ahiam son of Sachar the Hararite, Eliphal son of Ur,
36 Hepher the Mecherathite, Ahijah the Pelonite,
37 Hezro the Carmelite, Naarai son of Ezbai,
38 Joel the brother of Nathan, Mibhar son of Hagri,
39 Zelek the Ammonite, Naharai the Beerothite (the weapon carrier of Joab son of Zeruiah),
40 Ira the Ithrite, Gareb the Ithrite,
41 Uriah the Hittite, Zabad son of Ahlai,
42 Adina son of Shiza the Reubenite (a chief of the Reubenites, with thirty men under him),
43 Hanan son of Maacah, Joshaphat the Mithnite,
44 Uzzia the Ashterathite, Shama and Jeiel the sons of Hotham the Aroerite,
45 Jediael son of Shimri and his brother Joha the Tizite,
46 Eliel the Mahavite, Jeribai and Joshaviah the sons of Elnaam, Ithmah the Moabite,
47 Eliel, Obed, and Jaasiel the Mezobaite.

title: 1 Chronicles 12
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 These are the men who came to David at Ziklag. He was still sent away from Saul son of Kish. They were strong men who helped him in war.
2 They were bowmen. They could throw stones and shoot arrows with both hands. They were Saul's relatives from Benjamin.
3 Their chief was Ahiezer, with Joash. They were sons of Shemaah the Gibeathite. Also Jeziel and Pelet, sons of Azmaveth. Also Beracah and Jehu the Anathothite.
4 Then Ishmaiah the Gibeonite, a strong man among the Thirty and a leader of the Thirty. Also Jeremiah, Jahaziel, Johanan, and Jozabad the Gederathite.
5 Then Eluzai, Jerimoth, Bealiah, Shemariah, and Shephatiah the Haruphite.
6 Then Elkanah, Isshiah, Azarel, Joezer, and Jashobeam. They were Korahites.
7 And Joelah and Zebadiah, the sons of Jeroham from Gedor.

8 Some men of Gad left Saul and joined David at his safe place in the desert. They were brave strong men, trained for war. They knew how to use the shield and spear. Their faces were like lion faces. They were fast as deer on the mountains.
9 Ezer was the chief. Obadiah was second. Eliab was third.
10 Mishmannah was fourth. Jeremiah was fifth.
11 Attai was sixth. Eliel was seventh.
12 Johanan was eighth. Elzabad was ninth.
13 Jeremiah was tenth. Machbanai was eleventh.
14 These men of Gad were army leaders. The smallest one was as strong as a hundred men. The greatest was as strong as a thousand.
15 They crossed the Jordan in the first month. The river was full and went over its banks. They made all the people in the valleys run away, both east and west.
16 Other men from Benjamin and Judah also came to David at his safe place.

17 David went out to meet them. He said, "If you come in peace to help me, my heart will join with you. But if you come to hand me over to my enemies when I have done no wrong, may the God of our fathers see it and judge you."

18 Then the Spirit of God came on Amasai, the chief of the Thirty. He said: "We are yours, O David! We are with you, O son of Jesse! Peace, peace to you, and peace to your helpers, for your God helps you." So David received them and made them leaders of his troops.

19 Some men of Manasseh left Saul and joined David when he went with the Philistines to fight Saul. But David did not help the Philistines. Their rulers talked together and sent David away. They said, "It will cost us our heads if he goes back to his master Saul."
20 When David went to Ziklag, these men of Manasseh joined him: Adnah, Jozabad, Jediael, Michael, Jozabad, Elihu, and Zillethai. They were leaders of thousands in Manasseh.
21 They helped David fight against groups of robbers. They were all brave strong men and leaders in the army.
22 Day after day, men came to help David. Soon he had a great army, like God's own army.
23 These are the numbers of men ready for war who came to David at Hebron. They came to give Saul's kingdom to him, just as the LORD had said:
24 From Judah: 6,800 men ready for war with shields and spears.
25 From Simeon: 7,100 brave strong men, ready for war.
26 From Levi: 4,600,
27 including Jehoiada, the leader of Aaron's house, with 3,700 men,
28 and Zadok, a brave young strong man, with 22 leaders from his own family.
29 From Benjamin, Saul's relatives: 3,000. Most of them had stayed loyal to Saul's house until then.
30 From Ephraim: 20,800 brave strong men, well-known in their own clans.
31 From the half-tribe of Manasseh: 18,000 chosen by name to come and make David king.
32 From Issachar, men who understood the times and knew what Israel should do: 200 chiefs, with all their relatives under them.
33 From Zebulun: 50,000 men ready to serve. They were trained for war with all kinds of weapons. They had one heart for David.
34 From Naphtali: 1,000 leaders, with 37,000 men carrying shield and spear.
35 From Dan: 28,600 men ready for war.
36 From Asher: 40,000 men ready to serve and ready for war.
37 From east of the Jordan, from Reuben, Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh: 120,000 men with every kind of weapon of war.
38 All these men of war lined up for battle. They came to Hebron fully ready to make David king over all Israel. All the other Israelites also agreed to make David king.
39 They stayed there with David for three days, eating and drinking. Their relatives had given them food.

40 Their neighbors from as far as Issachar, Zebulun, and Naphtali brought a lot of food. They brought it on donkeys, camels, mules, and oxen. There was flour, fig cakes and raisin cakes, wine and oil, oxen and sheep. There was great joy in Israel.

title: 1 Chronicles 13
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then David talked with all his leaders, the leaders of hundreds and thousands.

2 He said to all the people of Israel, "If it seems good to you, and if this is what the LORD our God wants, let us send a message everywhere. Let us call the rest of our brothers in all the land of Israel. Let us also call the priests and Levites in their cities and open fields, so they may join us.
3 Then let us bring back the ark of our God. For we did not ask Him in the days of Saul."
4 This plan seemed right to all the people. So the whole group agreed to do it.
5 So David gathered all Israel, from the River Shihor in Egypt to Lebo-hamath. He went to bring the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim.

6 David and all Israel went up to Baalah of Judah (that is, Kiriath-jearim). They went to bring up the ark of God the LORD, who sits on His throne between the cherubim—the ark that is called by the Name.
7 They carried the ark of God from the house of Abinadab on a new cart. Uzzah and Ahio guided the cart.
8 David and all the Israelites were celebrating before God with all their strength. They sang songs and played harps and lyres, with tambourines, cymbals, and trumpets.
9 When they came to the threshing floor of Chidon, Uzzah reached out and took hold of the ark. He did this because the oxen had stumbled.
10 The anger of the LORD was very hot against Uzzah. He killed him because he had put his hand on the ark. So Uzzah died there before God.
11 Then David became angry because the LORD had broken out against Uzzah. So he named that place Perez-uzzah, as it is called to this day.
12 That day David feared God. He asked, "How can I ever bring the ark of God to me?"
13 So he did not move the ark with him to the City of David. Instead, he took it aside to the house of Obed-edom the Gittite.
14 So the ark of God stayed with the family of Obed-edom in his house for three months. And the LORD blessed his family and everything he owned.

title: 1 Chronicles 14
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Hiram, the king of Tyre, sent messengers to David. He also sent cedar wood, workers who cut stone, and workers who build with wood. They came to build a royal house for David.
2 Then David knew that the LORD had made him king over Israel. The LORD had made his kingdom very great because of His people Israel.
3 In Jerusalem David took more wives. He had more sons and more daughters.
4 These are the names of the children born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
5 Ibhar, Elishua, Elpelet,
6 Nogah, Nepheg, Japhia,
7 Elishama, Beeliada, and Eliphelet.
8 The Philistines heard that David was chosen as king over all Israel. So they all went to look for him. But David heard about it and went out to meet them.
9 The Philistines had come and attacked the Valley of Rephaim.
10 So David asked God, “Should I go up to fight the Philistines? Will You give them to me?” The LORD answered, “Go. I will give them to you.”

11 So David and his men went up to Baal-perazim. There David defeated the Philistines. He said, “Like a sudden flood of water, God has broken through against my enemies by my hand.” So they called that place Baal-perazim.
12 The Philistines left their gods behind there. David ordered his men to burn them in the fire.
13 The Philistines came again and attacked the valley.
14 So David asked God again. God answered him, “Do not go straight up after them. Go around them. Attack them in front of the balsam trees.
15 Listen for the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees. When you hear it, go out to fight. This will mean that God has gone out before you to attack the army of the Philistines.”
16 So David did what God had told him. They struck down the army of the Philistines all the way from Gibeon to Gezer.
17 So David’s name became known in every land. The LORD made all the nations afraid of him.

title: 1 Chronicles 15
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 David built houses for himself in the City of David. He also prepared a place for the ark of God and set up a tent for it.
2 Then David said, "Only the Levites may carry the ark of God. The LORD has chosen them to carry the ark of the LORD and to serve Him forever."
3 David called all Israel together in Jerusalem. He wanted to bring up the ark of the LORD to the place he had prepared.
4 Then he gathered the family members of Aaron and the Levites:
5 from the Kohathites, Uriel the chief and 120 of his relatives;
6 from the Merarites, Asaiah the chief and 220 of his relatives;
7 from the Gershomites, Joel the chief and 130 of his relatives;
8 from the Elizaphanites, Shemaiah the chief and 200 of his relatives;
9 from the Hebronites, Eliel the chief and 80 of his relatives;
10 from the Uzzielites, Amminadab the chief and 112 of his relatives.
11 David called the priests Zadok and Abiathar. He also called the Levites Uriel, Asaiah, Joel, Shemaiah, Eliel, and Amminadab.

12 He said to them, "You are the heads of the Levite families. You and your relatives must consecrate yourselves. Then you may bring the ark of the LORD, the God of Israel, to the place I have prepared."
13 "The first time, you Levites were not with us. So the LORD our God became very angry with us. For we did not ask Him the right way."
14 So the priests and Levites consecrated themselves. They would bring up the ark of the LORD, the God of Israel.
15 The Levites carried the ark of God on their shoulders with the poles. They did this as Moses had commanded, just as the LORD had said.
16 David also told the leaders of the Levites to choose their relatives as singers. They would lift up their voices with joy, with musical instruments—harps, lyres, and cymbals.
17 So the Levites chose Heman son of Joel. From his brothers, they chose Asaph son of Berechiah. From their brothers the Merarites, they chose Ethan son of Kushaiah.
18 With them were their brothers of lower rank: Zechariah, Jaaziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, and the gatekeepers Obed-edom and Jeiel.
19 The musicians Heman, Asaph, and Ethan were to sound the bronze cymbals.
20 Zechariah, Aziel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Unni, Eliab, Maaseiah, and Benaiah were to play the harps according to Alamoth.
21 Mattithiah, Eliphelehu, Mikneiah, Obed-edom, Jeiel, and Azaziah were to lead the music with lyres according to Sheminith.
22 Chenaniah was the head Levite and the director of the music. He was very skilled.
23 Berechiah and Elkanah were to be guards for the ark.
24 The priests Shebaniah, Joshaphat, Nethanel, Amasai, Zechariah, Benaiah, and Eliezer were to blow the trumpets before the ark of God. Obed-edom and Jehiah were also guards for the ark.
25 So David, the elders of Israel, and the leaders of one thousand men went with great joy. They went to bring the ark of the covenant of the LORD from the house of Obed-edom.
26 Because God helped the Levites who were carrying the ark of the covenant of the LORD, they offered seven bulls and seven rams as a sacrifice.

27 David was dressed in a robe of fine linen. All the Levites who carried the ark were also dressed this way. The singers and Chenaniah, the director of music, were dressed this way too. David also wore a linen ephod.
28 So all Israel brought up the ark of the covenant of the LORD with shouting. They sounded rams' horns and trumpets. They played cymbals, harps, and lyres.
29 The ark of the covenant of the LORD was entering the City of David. Saul's daughter Michal looked down from a window. She saw King David dancing and celebrating. And she hated him in her heart.

title: 1 Chronicles 16
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 So they brought the ark of God in. They placed it inside the tent that David had set up for it. Then they offered burnt offerings and peace offerings before God.
2 When David finished offering the burnt offerings and peace offerings, he blessed the people in the name of the LORD.
3 Then he gave to every man and woman of Israel a loaf of bread, a cake of dates, and a cake of raisins.
4 David chose some of the Levites to serve before the ark of the LORD. They were to celebrate, to give thanks, and to praise the LORD, the God of Israel.

5 Asaph was the chief, and Zechariah was second. Then came Jeiel, Shemiramoth, Jehiel, Mattithiah, Eliab, Benaiah, Obed-edom, and Jeiel. They were to play the harps and lyres, while Asaph sounded the cymbals.
6 The priests Benaiah and Jahaziel blew the trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of God.
7 On that day David first gave to Asaph and his brothers this song of thanksgiving to the LORD:
8 "Give thanks to the LORD; call on His name; tell the nations what He has done.
9 Sing to Him, sing praises to Him; tell of all His wonders.
10 Be proud of His holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the LORD be glad.
11 Seek the LORD and His strength; seek His face always.
12 Remember the wonders He has done, His amazing works, and the rulings He has spoken,
13 O children of His servant Israel, O sons of Jacob, His chosen ones.
14 He is the LORD our God; His rulings go out through all the earth.
15 Remember His covenant forever, the word He commanded for a thousand generations—
16 the covenant He made with Abraham, and the oath He swore to Isaac.
17 He made it firm for Jacob as a law, for Israel as an everlasting covenant:
18 'I will give you the land of Canaan as the share you will own.'
19 When they were few in number, very few, and strangers in the land,
20 they moved from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another.
21 He let no man hurt them; He warned kings for their sake:
22 'Do not touch the ones I have chosen! Do no harm to My prophets!'
23 Sing to the LORD, all the earth. Tell of His salvation day after day.
24 Tell of His glory among the nations, His wonderful works among all peoples.
25 For great is the LORD, and worthy of much praise; He is to be feared above all gods.
26 For all the gods of the nations are idols, but the LORD made the heavens.
27 Great beauty and power are before Him; strength and joy fill the place where He lives.
28 Give to the LORD, O families of the nations, give to the LORD glory and strength.
29 Give to the LORD the glory due His name; bring an offering and come before Him. Worship the LORD in His holy beauty;
30 shake with fear before Him, all the earth. The world is made firm; it cannot be moved.
31 Let the heavens be glad, and the earth rejoice. Let them say among the nations, 'The LORD reigns!'
32 Let the sea roar, and all that fills it; let the fields be glad, and all that is in them.
33 Then the trees of the forest will sing for joy before the LORD, for He is coming to judge the earth.
34 Give thanks to the LORD, for He is good; His loving devotion endures forever.
35 Then cry out: 'Save us, O God of our salvation; gather us and rescue us from the nations, that we may give thanks to Your holy name, that we may be proud of Your praise.'
36 Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel, from everlasting to everlasting." Then all the people said, "Amen!" and "Praise the LORD!"
37 So David left Asaph and his brothers there before the ark of the covenant of the LORD. They were to serve there regularly, by the daily needs of each day.
38 With them were Obed-edom and his sixty-eight relatives. Obed-edom son of Jeduthun, and also Hosah, were to be gatekeepers.
39 David left Zadok the priest and his fellow priests before the tent of worship of the LORD at the high place in Gibeon.
40 They were to offer burnt offerings to the LORD on the altar of burnt offerings every morning and evening. They did this just as the Law of the LORD had commanded Israel to keep.
41 With them were Heman, Jeduthun, and the rest who were chosen and called by name. They were to give thanks to the LORD, for "His loving devotion endures forever."
42 Heman and Jeduthun had trumpets and cymbals for the music. They also had instruments for the songs of God. And the sons of Jeduthun stood at the gate.
43 Then all the people went back to their homes. And David returned home to bless his household.

title: 1 Chronicles 17
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 After David had settled in his palace, he said to Nathan the prophet, "Here I am, living in a house of cedar. But the ark of the covenant of the LORD is under a tent."
2 Nathan replied to David, "Do all that is in your heart, for God is with you."
3 But that night the word of God came to Nathan. He said,
4 "Go and tell My servant David, 'This is what the LORD says: You are not the one to build Me a house to live in.
5 I have not lived in a house from the day I brought Israel up out of Egypt until now. I have moved from one tent to another.
6 In all My travels with all the Israelites, did I ever ask any of the leaders, "Why have you not built Me a house of cedar?" I had appointed them to shepherd My people.'
7 Now then, tell My servant David, 'This is what the LORD of Hosts says: I took you from the fields, where you followed the sheep, to be the ruler over My people Israel.
8 I have been with you wherever you have gone. I have defeated all your enemies before you. Now I will make for you a name like the greatest in the land.

9 I will provide a place for My people Israel. I will plant them there, so they may live in their own place. They will not be troubled anymore. Wicked people will no longer oppress them as they did at first,
10 and as they did from the day I appointed judges over My people Israel. I will defeat all your enemies. Also, I tell you that the LORD will build a house for you.
11 When your life is over and you join your fathers in death, I will raise up your offspring after you, one of your own sons. And I will establish his kingdom.
12 He will build a house for Me, and I will establish his throne forever.
13 I will be his Father, and he will be My son. And I will never take away My faithful love from him, as I took it from the one before you.
14 But I will set him over My house and My kingdom forever. His throne will be established forever.'"
15 So Nathan told David all the words of this entire vision.
16 Then King David went in and sat before the LORD. He said, "Who am I, O LORD God? And what is my house, that You have brought me to this point?
17 And as if this was a small thing in Your eyes, O God, You have spoken about the future of the house of Your servant. You have treated me as a man of high honor, O LORD God.
18 What more can David say to You for so honoring Your servant? For You know Your servant,
19 O LORD. For the sake of Your servant and according to Your own heart, You have done this great thing. You have shown all Your greatness.
20 O LORD, there is no one like You. There is no God but You, just as we have heard with our own ears.

21 And who is like Your people Israel? They are the one nation on earth whom God went out to redeem as a people for Himself. You made a name for Yourself through great and mighty wonders. You drove out nations from before Your people, whom You redeemed from Egypt.
22 You have made Your people Israel Your very own forever. And You, O LORD, have become their God.
23 And now, O LORD, let the word You have spoken about Your servant and his house be established forever. Do as You have promised.

24 Then Your name will be established and made great forever. People will say, 'The LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel, is God over Israel.' And may the house of Your servant David be established before You.
25 For You, my God, have shown Your servant that You will build a house for him. So Your servant has had the courage to pray before You.
26 And now, O LORD, You are God! You have promised this good thing to Your servant.
27 So now You have been pleased to bless the house of Your servant. May it continue forever before You. For You, O LORD, have blessed it. And it will be blessed forever."

title: 1 Chronicles 18
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Some time later, David defeated the Philistines. He brought them under his rule. He took the city of Gath and its villages from them.
2 David also defeated the Moabites. They became David's servants and brought him gifts of money.
3 David also defeated King Hadadezer of Zobah, near Hamath. Hadadezer had gone out to take control of land along the Euphrates River.
4 David took from him a thousand chariots, seven thousand chariot drivers, and twenty thousand foot soldiers. He cut the leg muscles of all the horses. He kept only a hundred for the chariots.
5 The Aramean people of Damascus came to help King Hadadezer of Zobah. David struck down twenty-two thousand of their men.
6 Then David placed groups of soldiers in Aram of Damascus. The Aramean people became David's servants and brought him gifts of money. So the LORD gave David victory wherever he went.
7 David took the gold shields that the officers of Hadadezer carried. He brought them to Jerusalem.
8 From Tibhath and Cun, which were cities of Hadadezer, David took a large amount of bronze. Later, Solomon used this bronze to make the bronze Sea, the pillars, and other bronze tools.
9 King Tou of Hamath heard that David had defeated the whole army of Hadadezer king of Zobah.
10 So he sent his son Hadoram to greet King David and to bless him. David had fought and defeated Hadadezer, who had been at war with Tou. Hadoram brought many gifts of gold, silver, and bronze.
11 King David gave these gifts to the LORD. He also gave the silver and gold he had taken from all these nations—from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, and the Amalekites.
12 Also, Abishai son of Zeruiah struck down eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
13 He placed groups of soldiers in Edom. All the Edomites became David's servants. So the LORD gave David victory wherever he went.
14 So David ruled over all Israel. He judged in a fair and right way for all his people.
15 Joab son of Zeruiah was leader of the army. Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the man who kept records.
16 Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests. Shavsha was the writer for the king.
17 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was leader over the Cherethites and Pelethites. David's sons were top helpers next to the king.

title: 1 Chronicles 19
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Some time later, Nahash king of the Ammonites died. His son became king after him.

2 David said, “I will be kind to Hanun son of Nahash. His father was kind to me.” So David sent men to comfort Hanun about his father. David’s servants arrived in the land of the Ammonites to comfort him.

3 But the leaders of the Ammonites said to Hanun, “David sent you these men. Do you really think he wants to honor your father? His servants have come to check your land. They want to spy on it and take it.”
4 So Hanun took David’s servants. He shaved their beards. He cut their clothes off at the hips. Then he sent them away.
5 Someone came and told David about his men. David sent messengers to meet them, because the men felt deep shame. The king said, “Stay in Jericho until your beards grow back. Then come home.”

6 The Ammonites saw that they were now hated by David. So Hanun and the Ammonites sent a thousand talents of silver. They used it to hire war carts and horse soldiers from Aram-naharaim, Aram-maacah, and Zobah.
7 They hired thirty-two thousand war carts. They also hired the king of Maacah and his troops. These men camped near Medeba. The Ammonites came out from their cities and marched to fight.
8 When David heard this, he sent Joab and the whole army of strong fighters.
9 The Ammonites marched out and stood in lines to fight at the city gate. The kings who came to help stood by themselves in the open fields.
10 Joab saw enemy lines in front of him and behind him. He chose the best men of Israel. He set them in lines against the Arameans.
11 He gave the rest of the army to his brother Abishai. Abishai set them in lines against the Ammonites.
12 Joab said, “If the Arameans are too strong for me, you will come and help me. If the Ammonites are too strong for you, I will come and help you.
13 Be strong. Let us fight bravely for our people and for the cities of our God. May the LORD do what is good in His sight.”
14 So Joab and his troops moved forward to fight the Arameans. The Arameans ran away from him.
15 When the Ammonites saw that the Arameans had run away, they also ran away from Abishai. They went back into the city. Then Joab returned to Jerusalem.
16 The Arameans saw that Israel had beaten them. They sent messengers to bring more Arameans from beyond the Euphrates River. Shophach, the leader of Hadadezer’s army, led them.
17 When David heard this, he gathered all Israel. He crossed the Jordan and moved toward the Arameans. He set his army in lines against them. David lined up to fight, and they fought against him.
18 But the Arameans ran away from Israel. David killed seven thousand of their chariot drivers and forty thousand soldiers on foot. He also killed Shophach, the leader of their army.
19 Hadadezer’s servants saw that Israel had beaten them. They made peace with David and obeyed him. So the Arameans would not help the Ammonites anymore.

title: 1 Chronicles 20
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 In the spring, when kings usually go out to fight, Joab led the army out. He destroyed the land of the Ammonites. He came to Rabbah and surrounded it with soldiers. But David stayed in Jerusalem. Joab attacked Rabbah and tore it down.

2 Then David took the crown from their king’s head. It weighed a talent of gold (about 34 kg). It was covered with valuable jewels. They placed it on David’s head. David also took many goods from the city.
3 David brought out the people who lived there. He put them to work with saws, iron picks, and axes. He did the same in every Ammonite city. Then David and all his soldiers went back to Jerusalem.
4 Some time later, war started with the Philistines at Gezer. At that time Sibbecai the Hushathite killed Sippai. Sippai was from the Rephaim family of giants. So the Philistines were defeated.
5 There was another battle with the Philistines. Elhanan son of Jair killed Lahmi. Lahmi was the brother of Goliath the Gittite. His spear shaft was like a weaver’s heavy beam.
6 There was still another battle at Gath. A very tall man lived there. He had six fingers on each hand and six toes on each foot. That made twenty-four in all. He also came from Rapha.
7 When he mocked Israel, Jonathan killed him. Jonathan was the son of David’s brother Shimei.
8 So these sons of Rapha in Gath were killed by David and his servants.

title: 1 Chronicles 21
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then Satan rose up against Israel. He pushed David to count the people of Israel.
2 So David said to Joab and the army leaders, "Go and count the Israelites from Beersheba to Dan. Bring me the number."
3 But Joab said, "May the LORD make His army a hundred times bigger. My lord the king, are they not all your servants? Why do you want to do this? Why bring guilt on Israel?"
4 But the king's word was stronger than Joab's. So Joab left and went through all Israel. Then he came back to Jerusalem.
5 Joab told David the total count. In all Israel there were 1,100,000 men who could fight with a sword. In Judah there were 470,000.
6 But Joab did not count Levi and Benjamin. The king's order was very hateful to him.
7 This order was also evil in God's sight. So He struck Israel.
8 Then David said to God, "I have sinned very much by doing this. Now please take away the sin of Your servant. I have acted very foolishly."
9 And the LORD spoke to Gad, David's prophet.
10 He said, "Go and tell David: This is what the LORD says. I am giving you three choices. Choose one, and I will do it to you."
11 So Gad went and said to David, "This is what the LORD says. You must choose

12 between three years with no food, or three months of running from your enemies while their swords catch you, or three days of the sword of the LORD. These would be days of plague on the land. The angel of the LORD would destroy every part of Israel. Now decide how I should answer Him who sent me."
13 David said to Gad, "I am deeply upset. Please let me fall into the hand of the LORD. His mercy is very great. But do not let me fall into the hands of men."
14 So the LORD sent a plague on Israel. Seventy thousand men of Israel fell dead.

15 Then God sent an angel to destroy Jerusalem. But as the angel was doing it, the LORD saw it and felt sorry about the disaster. He said to the angel who was destroying the people, "Enough! Pull back your hand now!" At that time the angel of the LORD was standing by the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.

16 David lifted up his eyes and saw the angel of the LORD. The angel was standing between heaven and earth. He had a drawn sword in his hand, stretched out over Jerusalem. David and the elders wore rough cloth and fell facedown.

17 And David said to God, "Was it not I who told them to count the people? I am the one who sinned and did wrong. But these sheep, what have they done? O LORD my God, please let Your hand fall on me and my father's family. But do not let this plague stay on Your people."
18 Then the angel of the LORD told Gad to tell David. David must go up and build an altar to the LORD on the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite.
19 So David went up at the word that Gad had spoken in the name of the LORD.
20 Now Ornan was threshing wheat. He turned and saw the angel. His four sons who were with him hid themselves.
21 David came to Ornan. When Ornan looked out and saw David, he left the threshing floor. He bowed facedown before David.
22 Then David said to Ornan, "Give me this threshing floor. I want to build an altar to the LORD here. Sell it to me for the full price. Then the plague on the people may stop."

23 Ornan said to David, "My lord the king, take whatever seems good. Look, I will give the oxen for the burnt offerings. I will give the threshing sledges for the wood. I will give the wheat for the grain offering. I will give it all."
24 "No," King David said. "I must pay the full price. I will not take for the LORD what belongs to you. I will not offer burnt offerings that cost me nothing."
25 So David paid Ornan six hundred shekels of gold for the site.
26 And there he built an altar to the LORD. He offered burnt offerings and peace offerings. He called on the LORD. The LORD answered him with fire from heaven on the altar of burnt offering.
27 Then the LORD spoke to the angel. The angel put his sword back into its cover.
28 At that time David saw that the LORD had answered him at the threshing floor of Ornan the Jebusite. So he offered sacrifices there.
29 The tent of the LORD that Moses had made in the wilderness was at the high place in Gibeon. The altar of burnt offering was also there.
30 But David could not go before it to ask God. He was afraid of the sword of the angel of the LORD.

title: 1 Chronicles 22
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then David said, "The house of the LORD God will be here. The altar for burnt offerings for Israel will also be here."
2 So David gave orders to gather the foreigners living in the land of Israel. He chose stonecutters from them. They cut and shaped stones to build the house of God.
3 David also got a large amount of iron. It was for nails for the gate doors and for the parts that join things. He got so much bronze that no one could weigh it.
4 He got more cedar logs than people could count. The Sidonians and the Tyrians brought a large amount of cedar logs to David.

5 David said, "My son Solomon is young and has not done this work before. The house for the LORD must be very great and beautiful. It must be famous and praised in every land. So I must get things ready for it." So David got many things ready before he died.
6 Then David called for his son Solomon. He told him to build a house for the LORD, the God of Israel.
7 David said to Solomon, "My son, I wanted in my heart to build a house for the Name of the LORD my God.

8 But this word of the LORD came to me. He said, 'You have killed many people. You have fought many big wars. You will not build a house for My Name. You have killed too many people on the ground before Me.
9 But a son will be born to you. He will be a man of peace. I will give him rest from all his enemies on every side. His name will be Solomon. I will give Israel peace and quiet while he rules.
10 He will build a house for My Name. He will be My son, and I will be his Father. I will make his kingdom over Israel strong forever.'
11 Now, my son, may the LORD be with you. May you succeed in building the house of the LORD your God, as He said.
12 Above all, may the LORD give you wisdom and understanding when He makes you king over Israel. Then you can keep the Law of the LORD your God.
13 You will succeed if you carefully follow the rules and laws that the LORD gave Moses for Israel. Be strong and brave. Do not be afraid. Do not lose hope.

14 Look, I have worked very hard for the house of the LORD. I have given 100,000 talents of gold and 1,000,000 talents of silver. I have given so much bronze and iron that no one can weigh it. I have also given wood and stone. You can add more to them.
15 You also have many workers. You have stonecutters, builders who work with stone, carpenters, and men skilled in every kind of work.
16 They work with gold, silver, bronze, and iron. You have more skilled workers than you can count. Now begin the work. May the LORD be with you."
17 Then David told all the leaders of Israel to help his son Solomon.

18 He said, "Is not the LORD your God with you? Has He not given you rest on every side? He has given the people of this land into my hand. The land has been brought under the power of the LORD and His people.

19 Now give your whole heart and soul to seek the LORD your God. Start building the sanctuary of the LORD God. Then you can bring the ark of the covenant of the LORD and the holy items of God into the temple. The temple will be built for the Name of the LORD."

title: 1 Chronicles 23
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 When David was old and had lived many years, he made his son Solomon king over Israel.
