2 Then he gathered all the leaders of Israel, with the priests and Levites.
3 The Levites who were thirty years old or older were counted. The total was 38,000 men.
4 David said, “Of these, 24,000 will watch over the work of the house of the LORD. 6,000 will be leaders and judges.
5 4,000 will be gatekeepers. And 4,000 will praise the LORD with the music tools I have made.”
6 Then David put the Levites into groups by the sons of Levi: Gershom, Kohath, and Merari.
7 The Gershonites: Ladan and Shimei.
8 The sons of Ladan: Jehiel the first, Zetham, and Joel—three in all.
9 The sons of Shimei: Shelomoth, Haziel, and Haran—three in all. These were the heads of the families of Ladan.
10 And the sons of Shimei: Jahath, Zina, Jeush, and Beriah. These were the sons of Shimei—four in all.
11 Jahath was the first and Zizah was the second. But Jeush and Beriah did not have many sons. So they were counted as one family with one job.
12 The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel—four in all.

13 The sons of Amram: Aaron and Moses. Aaron and his sons after him were set apart forever to take care of the most holy things, to burn incense before the LORD, to serve Him, and to speak blessings in His name forever.
14 As for Moses the man of God, his sons were counted in the tribe of Levi.
15 The sons of Moses: Gershom and Eliezer.
16 The family of Gershom: Shebuel was the first.
17 The family of Eliezer: Rehabiah was the first. Eliezer had no other sons, but the sons of Rehabiah were very many.
18 The sons of Izhar: Shelomith was the first.
19 The sons of Hebron: Jeriah was the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
20 The sons of Uzziel: Micah was the first and Isshiah the second.
21 The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi.
22 The sons of Mahli: Eleazar and Kish. Eleazar died with no sons; he had only daughters. Their cousins, the sons of Kish, married them.
23 The sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jeremoth—three in all.
24 These were the family line of Levi. They were heads of families, listed one by one by name. They were twenty years old or older and worked in the service of the house of the LORD.
25 For David had said, “The LORD, the God of Israel, has given peace to His people and now lives in Jerusalem forever.
26 So now the Levites do not need to carry the tabernacle or any of the tools used in worship.”
27 By the last orders of David, the Levites twenty years old or older were counted.
28 Their job was to help the sons of Aaron with the service of the house of the LORD. They cared for the courtyards and rooms, the cleaning of all the holy things, and the work of the house of God.
29 They also cared for the rows of showbread, the fine flour for the grain offering, the thin bread made without yeast, the baking, the mixing, and all measures of size and amount.
30 Every morning they stood to give thanks and praise to the LORD. They did the same in the evening.
31 When burnt offerings were given to the LORD on Sabbaths, New Moons, and set feasts, they served before the LORD in the right numbers.
32 So the Levites took care of the Tent of Meeting and the Holy Place. Under their brothers, the sons of Aaron, they did the service of the house of the LORD.

title: 1 Chronicles 24
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 These were the groups of the sons of Aaron. The sons of Aaron were Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
2 But Nadab and Abihu died before their father, and they had no sons. So Eleazar and Ithamar served as priests.
3 Helped by Zadok from Eleazar’s family and Ahimelech from Ithamar’s family, David put them into groups by the jobs of their service.
4 More leaders were found among Eleazar’s sons than among Ithamar’s. So they were divided in this way. There were sixteen family heads from Eleazar’s sons and eight from Ithamar’s sons.
5 They were grouped by drawing lots, because there were leaders of the sanctuary and leaders of God from both Eleazar’s and Ithamar’s sons.

6 The scribe Shemaiah son of Nethanel, a Levite, wrote down their names. He did this in front of the king, the leaders, Zadok the priest, Ahimelech son of Abiathar, and the family heads of the priests and the Levites. One family was taken from Eleazar, then one from Ithamar.
7 The first lot went to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedaiah,
8 the third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,
9 the fifth to Malchijah, the sixth to Mijamin,
10 the seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Abijah,
11 the ninth to Jeshua, the tenth to Shecaniah,
12 the eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,
13 the thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Jeshebeab,
14 the fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,
15 the seventeenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Happizzez,
16 the nineteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Jehezkel,
17 the twenty-first to Jachin, the twenty-second to Gamul,
18 the twenty-third to Delaiah, and the twenty-fourth to Maaziah.
19 This was their set order for service when they came into the house of the LORD. They followed the rules given to them by their father long ago, Aaron, as the LORD, the God of Israel, had told him.
20 Now these were the other family line of Levi: From the sons of Amram: Shubael; from the sons of Shubael: Jehdeiah.
21 As for Rehabiah, from his sons: The first was Isshiah.
22 From the Izharites: Shelomoth; from the sons of Shelomoth: Jahath.
23 From the sons of Hebron: Jeriah was the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
24 From the sons of Uzziel: Micah; from the sons of Micah: Shamir.
25 The brother of Micah: Isshiah; from the sons of Isshiah: Zechariah.
26 The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. The son of Jaaziah: Beno.
27 The family of Merari from Jaaziah: Beno, Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.
28 From Mahli: Eleazar, who had no sons.
29 From Kish: Jerahmeel the son of Kish.
30 And the sons of Mushi: Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the sons of the Levites, by their families.

31 Just like their brothers the sons of Aaron, they also drew lots in front of King David, Zadok, Ahimelech, and the family heads of the priests and Levites. The family heads and their younger brothers did this in the same way.

title: 1 Chronicles 25
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then David and the army leaders chose some sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun for special work. They would prophesy (speak God's words) while playing lyres, harps, and cymbals. Here is the list of the men who did this work:
2 From the sons of Asaph: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asarelah. These sons of Asaph were led by Asaph, who prophesied as the king directed.
3 From the sons of Jeduthun: Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah—six in all. Their father Jeduthun led them. He prophesied with the harp and thanked and praised the LORD.
4 From the sons of Heman: Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, Romamti-ezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth.
5 All these were sons of Heman, the king's seer (a man who saw God's messages). God gave him these sons as he had promised, to lift him up. God gave Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
6 All of them were led by their fathers. They made music in the house of the LORD with cymbals, harps, and lyres. They served in the house of God. Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman were led by the king.
7 With their relatives, they were all well trained and skilled in the songs of the LORD. There were 288 of them.
8 They cast lots (chose by drawing) for their duties. Both young and old took part, both teacher and student.
9 The first lot went to Joseph, of Asaph's family, with his sons and brothers—12 in all. The second went to Gedaliah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
10 The third went to Zaccur, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
11 The fourth went to Izri, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
12 The fifth went to Nethaniah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
13 The sixth went to Bukkiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
14 The seventh went to Jesarelah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
15 The eighth went to Jeshaiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
16 The ninth went to Mattaniah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
17 The tenth went to Shimei, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
18 The eleventh went to Azarel, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
19 The twelfth went to Hashabiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
20 The thirteenth went to Shubael, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
21 The fourteenth went to Mattithiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
22 The fifteenth went to Jeremoth, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
23 The sixteenth went to Hananiah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
24 The seventeenth went to Joshbekashah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
25 The eighteenth went to Hanani, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
26 The nineteenth went to Mallothi, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
27 The twentieth went to Eliathah, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
28 The twenty-first went to Hothir, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
29 The twenty-second went to Giddalti, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
30 The twenty-third went to Mahazioth, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.
31 The twenty-fourth went to Romamti-ezer, with his sons and brothers—12 in all.

title: 1 Chronicles 26
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 These were the groups of the gatekeepers. From the Korahites: Meshelemiah son of Kore, from the sons of Asaph.
2 Meshelemiah had sons: Zechariah the first, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,
3 Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, and Eliehoenai the seventh.
4 Obed-edom also had sons: Shemaiah the first, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, Sachar the fourth, Nethanel the fifth,
5 Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, and Peullethai the eighth. For God had blessed Obed-edom.
6 His son Shemaiah also had sons. They led their families, because they were strong and able men.
7 Shemaiah’s sons were Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad. His brothers Elihu and Semachiah were also able men.
8 All these came from Obed-edom. They, their sons, and their brothers were able men with strength to work — 62 in all.
9 Meshelemiah also had sons and brothers who were able men — 18 in all.
10 Hosah the Merarite also had sons: Shimri was the first. He was not the oldest, but his father made him the first.
11 Hilkiah was the second, Tebaliah the third, and Zechariah the fourth. Hosah’s sons and brothers were 13 in all.
12 These groups of gatekeepers, led by their chief men, had duties to serve in the house of the LORD, like their brothers.
13 They threw lots for each gate, by their families, young and old alike.
14 The lot for the East Gate fell to Shelemiah. Then they threw lots for his son Zechariah, a wise adviser, and the North Gate fell to him.
15 The lot for the South Gate fell to Obed-edom, and the storerooms fell to his sons.
16 The lots for the West Gate and the Shallecheth Gate, on the road that goes up, fell to Shuppim and Hosah. Guards stood at every post.
17 Each day there were six Levites on the east, four on the north, four on the south, and two pairs at the storerooms.
18 At the court on the west, there were four men at the road and two at the court.
19 These were the groups of the gatekeepers from the sons of Korah and Merari.
20 Their fellow Levites were in charge of the storerooms of the house of God and the storerooms for things given to God.
21 From the sons of Ladan the Gershonite, who led the families of Ladan, came Jehieli,
22 the sons of Jehieli, Zetham, and his brother Joel. They were in charge of the storerooms of the house of the LORD.
23 From the Amramites, the Izharites, the Hebronites, and the Uzzielites:
24 Shebuel was a descendant of Gershom son of Moses. He was the chief officer over the storerooms.
25 His relatives through Eliezer were Rehabiah his son, Jeshaiah his son, Joram his son, Zichri his son, and Shelomith his son.

26 This Shelomith and his brothers were in charge of all the storerooms for things given to God. King David, the family leaders, the leaders of 1,000 and of 100 soldiers, and the army leaders had given these things.
27 They had given some of the things taken in war to repair the house of the LORD.
28 Everything given by Samuel the prophet, by Saul son of Kish, by Abner son of Ner, and by Joab son of Zeruiah, with all other gifts, was under the care of Shelomith and his brothers.
29 From the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons had outside duties. They worked as officers and judges over Israel.
30 From the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his relatives, 1,700 able men, were in charge of Israel west of the Jordan, for all the work of the LORD and the service of the king.

31 Jerijah was the chief of the Hebronites, by the family records of his ancestors. In the fortieth year of David’s rule, the records were searched, and strong, able men were found among the Hebronites at Jazer in Gilead.

32 Among Jerijah’s relatives were 2,700 able men, who were family leaders. King David put them in charge of the Reubenites, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh, for every matter about God and the work of the king.

title: 1 Chronicles 27
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 This is the list of the Israelites who served King David. They were the family leaders, the leaders of 1,000s and 100s, and the officers. They led army groups that took turns each month all year. Each group had 24,000 men.
2 Jashobeam son of Zabdiel led the first group, for the first month. His group had 24,000 men.
3 He was from the family of Perez. He was head of all the army leaders for the first month.
4 Dodai the Ahohite led the group for the second month. Mikloth was his second leader. His group had 24,000 men.
5 The third leader, for the third month, was Benaiah son of Jehoiada the priest. His group had 24,000 men.
6 This Benaiah was a great hero among the Thirty. He was head of the Thirty. His son Ammizabad led his group.
7 The fourth, for the fourth month, was Asahel, Joab's brother. His son Zebadiah took over after him. His group had 24,000 men.
8 The fifth, for the fifth month, was the leader Shamhuth the Izrahite. His group had 24,000 men.
9 The sixth, for the sixth month, was Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite. His group had 24,000 men.
10 The seventh, for the seventh month, was Helez the Pelonite, from the tribe of Ephraim. His group had 24,000 men.
11 The eighth, for the eighth month, was Sibbecai the Hushathite, a Zerahite. His group had 24,000 men.
12 The ninth, for the ninth month, was Abiezer the Anathothite, from the tribe of Benjamin. His group had 24,000 men.
13 The tenth, for the tenth month, was Maharai the Netophathite, a Zerahite. His group had 24,000 men.
14 The eleventh, for the eleventh month, was Benaiah the Pirathonite, from the tribe of Ephraim. His group had 24,000 men.
15 The twelfth, for the twelfth month, was Heldai the Netophathite, from the family of Othniel. His group had 24,000 men.
16 These officers led the tribes of Israel. Over the Reubenites was Eliezer son of Zichri. Over the Simeonites was Shephatiah son of Maacah.
17 Over Levi was Hashabiah son of Kemuel. Over Aaron was Zadok.
18 Over Judah was Elihu, one of David's brothers. Over Issachar was Omri son of Michael.
19 Over Zebulun was Ishmaiah son of Obadiah. Over Naphtali was Jerimoth son of Azriel.
20 Over the Ephraimites was Hoshea son of Azaziah. Over one half of the tribe of Manasseh was Joel son of Pedaiah.
21 Over the other half of Manasseh in Gilead was Iddo son of Zechariah. Over Benjamin was Jaasiel son of Abner.
22 And over Dan was Azarel son of Jeroham. These were the leaders of the tribes of Israel.
23 David did not count the men aged twenty or under. The LORD had said He would make Israel as many as the stars in the sky.
24 Joab son of Zeruiah began to count the men but did not finish. Because of this count, God's anger came on Israel. So the number was not written in the Book of the Chronicles of King David.
25 Azmaveth son of Adiel was in charge of the king's storehouses. Jonathan son of Uzziah was in charge of the storehouses in the country, in the cities, in the villages, and in the strong towers.
26 Ezri son of Chelub was in charge of the workers in the fields who worked the ground.
27 Shimei the Ramathite was in charge of the vineyards. Zabdi the Shiphmite was in charge of the grapes used to make wine.
28 Baal-hanan the Gederite was in charge of the olive and sycamore trees in the low hills. Joash was in charge of the stores of olive oil.
29 Shitrai the Sharonite was in charge of the cattle eating grass in Sharon. Shaphat son of Adlai was in charge of the cattle in the valleys.
30 Obil the Ishmaelite was in charge of the camels. Jehdeiah the Meronothite was in charge of the donkeys.
31 Jaziz the Hagrite was in charge of the flocks. All these men were in charge of King David's property.
32 David's uncle Jonathan was a counselor. He was a wise man who could read and write. Jehiel son of Hachmoni took care of the king's sons.
33 Ahithophel was the king's counselor. Hushai the Archite was the king's close friend.
34 Ahithophel was followed by Jehoiada son of Benaiah, and then by Abiathar. Joab was the commander of the king's army.

title: 1 Chronicles 28
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 David called together all the leaders of Israel to Jerusalem. He called the leaders of the tribes and the leaders of the king’s work groups. He called the leaders of large and small army groups. He called the officials over all the king’s property and animals. He also called the king’s sons, the court officials, and every strong and brave man.

2 Then King David stood up and said, “Listen to me, my brothers and my people. I wanted in my heart to build a house. It would be a place where the ark of the covenant of the LORD could rest. It would be a footstool for our God. I had made plans to build it.
3 But God said to me, ‘You must not build a house for My Name. You are a man of war, and you have killed many people.’

4 Yet the LORD, the God of Israel, chose me from all my father’s family. He made me king over Israel forever. He chose Judah as the leader. From Judah He chose my father’s family. From my father’s sons He was pleased to make me king over all Israel.
5 The LORD has given me many sons. Out of all my sons He has chosen Solomon. Solomon will sit on the throne of the LORD’s kingdom over Israel.
6 He said to me, ‘Solomon your son will build My house and My courts. I have chosen him as My son, and I will be his Father.
7 I will set up his kingdom forever. He must firmly obey My commandments and rules, as he is doing today.’

8 So now I speak before all Israel, the assembly of the LORD. Our God is listening. Keep and obey all the commandments of the LORD your God. Then you may keep this good land. You can pass it on as a gift to your children and their children forever.

9 As for you, Solomon my son, know the God of your father. Serve Him with all your heart and a willing mind. The LORD searches every heart. He understands every thought we have. If you seek Him, He will let you find Him. But if you leave Him, He will turn away from you forever.
10 Think about this now: the LORD has chosen you to build a house for the holy place. Be strong and do it.”

11 Then David gave his son Solomon the plans. The plans were for the front porch of the temple. They were for its buildings and storage rooms. They were for the upper rooms and inner rooms. They were also for the room for the mercy seat.

12 The plans had everything David had in his mind. They were for the courts of the house of the LORD. They were for all the rooms around it. They were for the rooms for treasure and for the gifts given to God.
13 The plans were for the work groups of the priests and Levites. They were for all the service work in the house of the LORD. They were for all the tools used in the house of the LORD.
14 David gave the weight for all the gold tools for every kind of service. He gave the weight for all the silver tools for every kind of service.
15 He gave the weight of the gold lampstands and their lamps. He gave the weight of each lampstand and its lamps. He gave the weight of each silver lampstand and its lamps, by their use.
16 He gave the weight of gold for each table of the special bread for God. He gave the weight of silver for the silver tables.
17 He gave the weight of pure gold for the forks, sprinkling bowls, and pitchers. He gave the weight of each gold dish. He gave the weight of each silver bowl.

18 He gave the weight of pure gold for the altar for burning incense. He gave the plans for the gold chariot of the cherubim. The cherubim spread their wings and covered the ark of the covenant of the LORD.
19 David said, “All of this, every part of the plan, the LORD made clear to me. He wrote it for me by His hand on me.”

20 David also said to Solomon his son, “Be strong and brave, and do the work. Do not be afraid or lose hope. The LORD God, my God, is with you. He will not fail you. He will not leave you until all the work for the house of the LORD is done.

21 The work groups of the priests and Levites are ready for all the service of the house of God. Every willing man with skill will help you in the work. The officials and all the people will do whatever you say.”

title: 1 Chronicles 29
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — 1 Chronicles
paragraphs: 1 Then King David said to all the people gathered, "My son Solomon is the one God has chosen. He is young and still learning. The work is very big. This great house is not for a man, but for the LORD God.

2 With all my strength I have prepared things for the house of my God. I have gold for the gold parts, silver for the silver, bronze for the bronze, iron for the iron, and wood for the wood. I also have onyx stones, turquoise, colored stones, all kinds of fine stones, and pieces of marble — very much of each.
3 Also, because I love the house of my God, I now give my own gold and silver for it. This is on top of all I have already given for this holy temple:
4 three thousand talents of gold from Ophir, and seven thousand talents of pure silver, to cover the walls of the buildings,
5 for the gold work, the silver work, and all the work done by the skilled men. Now who will give himself fully to the LORD today?"
6 Then the leaders of the families, the leaders of the tribes of Israel, the captains of thousands and of hundreds, and the officials over the king's work all gave with a glad heart.
7 For the work of God's house they gave 5,000 talents and 10,000 darics of gold, 10,000 talents of silver, 18,000 talents of bronze, and 100,000 talents of iron.
8 Anyone who had fine stones gave them to the treasury of the house of the LORD. Jehiel the Gershonite was in charge of them.
9 The people were glad because their leaders gave so freely. They had given to the LORD with full and glad hearts. And King David was very glad too.
10 Then David blessed the LORD in front of all the people gathered. He said: "Blessed are You, O LORD, God of our father Israel, for ever and ever.

11 Yours, O LORD, is the greatness, the power, the glory, the beauty, and the greatness. Everything in heaven and on earth belongs to You. Yours, O LORD, is the kingdom. You are lifted up as the head over all.
12 Riches and honor come from You. You rule over all. In Your hands are power and strength. You can lift up anyone and make anyone strong.
13 So now, our God, we give You thanks. We praise Your glorious name.
14 But who am I, and who are my people, that we should be able to give like this? For all things come from You. From Your own hand we have given to You.
15 We are foreigners and visitors before You, as all our fathers were. Our days on earth are like a shadow, without hope on our own.
16 O LORD our God, all this great supply for building You a house for Your holy Name comes from Your hand. All of it belongs to You.

17 I know, my God, that You test the heart. You are pleased with a right heart. I have given all these things with a glad and right heart. Now I have seen Your people here giving to You with joy and a glad heart.
18 O LORD, God of our fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, keep this desire in the hearts of Your people for ever. Turn their hearts toward You.
19 And give my son Solomon a whole heart to keep and obey all Your commands, rules, and laws, and to build the great house I have prepared for."

20 Then David said to all the people gathered, "Bless the LORD your God." So all the people gathered blessed the LORD, the God of their fathers. They bowed low and gave honor to the LORD and to the king.

21 The next day they offered sacrifices and brought burnt offerings to the LORD: a thousand bulls, a thousand rams, and a thousand lambs, with their drink offerings, and many other sacrifices for all Israel.
22 That day they ate and drank with great joy before the LORD. Then, a second time, they made David's son Solomon king, anointing him before the LORD as ruler, and Zadok as the priest.
23 So Solomon sat on the throne of the LORD as king in place of his father David. He did well, and all Israel obeyed him.
24 All the officials, the strong men, and all the sons of King David promised to obey King Solomon.
25 The LORD lifted Solomon up high in front of all Israel. He gave him royal greatness that no king in Israel had been given before.
26 David son of Jesse was king over all Israel.
27 David ruled over Israel for forty years — seven years in Hebron and thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
28 He died at a good old age, full of years, riches, and honor. His son Solomon ruled in his place.
29 Now the acts of King David, from first to last, are written in the Chronicles of Samuel the Seer, the Chronicles of Nathan the Prophet, and the Chronicles of Gad the Seer.
30 They tell about all his rule, his power, and the things that happened to him, to Israel, and to all the kingdoms of the lands.
